text.title: Perf Agent Workflow and Gotchas
text.summary: Agentic performance testing workflow, failure modes, CLI tools, and human-in-loop checkpoints.

section.title: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Step 1: Agent reads OpenAPI spec or route table and generates a weighted scenario file (k6 or Locust). Step 2: Agent runs a 30-second smoke at 5 VUs to confirm script correctness. Step 3: If smoke passes, agent runs the staged ramp. Step 4: Agent publishes JSON output for a review agent to compare against baseline.

rule.statement: Always smoke-test the generated script first. Agents that go straight to 1000 VUs waste cloud spend when the scenario is broken.
rule.rationale: A broken scenario at 1000 VUs costs real money and time. A smoke at 5 VUs costs 30 seconds.

antipattern.description: Agent copies BASE_URL hardcoded to production.
antipattern.reason: Force __ENV.BASE_URL in k6 and fail the script if unset. This prevents accidental load against prod.
Without check() + errorRate.add(1), the threshold output reads success even when the SUT is 5xx-ing. check() is mandatory for every response.

antipattern.description: Agent auto-tunes thresholds based on previous run results.
antipattern.reason: Convergence on the regressed value — the threshold degrades to match the slow system instead of catching the regression.

antipattern.description: Thresholds set too tight in CI (p95 < 200ms on staging).
antipattern.reason: Flakes that erode trust cause teams to relax all thresholds. Set realistic thresholds validated over 3+ runs before adding to CI gate.

rule.statement: Keep soak tests (8h, low load) and stress tests (3 min, ramp to break) in separate files. Never combine into one scenario.
rule.rationale: Agents conflate them. Soak finds memory leaks and connection pool exhaustion; stress finds throughput ceiling. They require different VU counts, durations, and thresholds.

section.title: CLI Tools
section.p: k6: modern JS load testing, single binary. brew install k6 / docker grafana/k6
locust: Python load testing with web UI. pip install locust
wrk / wrk2: low-overhead HTTP benchmarking. apt install wrk
vegeta: constant-rate Go HTTP attacker. go install github.com/tsenart/vegeta/v12@latest
pytest-benchmark: Python micro-benchmarks with regression alerts. pip install pytest-benchmark
hyperfine: CLI command benchmark with stats. cargo install hyperfine
oha: Rust wrk alternative with TUI. cargo install oha

rule.statement: Review the generated scenario weights and stage shape before running any test lasting more than 5 minutes. Cloud spend accumulates quickly at 100+ VUs.
rule.rationale: An agent that starts a 30-minute k6 cloud run with wrong weights or wrong target URL burns budget and produces useless data. The 30-second review before the run is the cheapest gate.
